faq.question: How do I get an accurate quotation?
faq.answer: Send your floor plan, area photos, rough measurements and preferred design. Siong Design can advise an estimated range before site measurement.

faq.question: Is the calculator price final?
faq.answer: No. It is for budgeting only. Final price depends on measurement, material, accessories, layout complexity and installation condition.

faq.question: How is kitchen cabinet pricing calculated?
faq.answer: Kitchen top cabinet and bottom cabinet are treated as separate items. For example, 10 ft top + 10 ft bottom means two separate 10 ft runs.

faq.question: What does per foot run mean?
faq.answer: Foot run usually refers to the horizontal length of the cabinet or wardrobe. Height, depth and internal layout may affect final price.

faq.question: Do you provide free measurement?
faq.answer: Yes, for suitable enquiries. Customers can WhatsApp photos and requirements first before arranging measurement.

faq.question: What materials can I choose?
faq.answer: Options may include standard laminate, premium laminate, low formaldehyde board and special finishes.

faq.question: What is included in the wardrobe promo?
faq.answer: The wardrobe promo is for selected casement wardrobe works from $220/ft. Terms and conditions apply.

faq.question: How long does fabrication take?
faq.answer: Timeline depends on project size, material availability and site readiness. A schedule is advised after confirmation.

faq.question: Can I customise the layout?
faq.answer: Yes. Shelves, drawers, hanging areas, display sections and LED lighting can be discussed.

faq.question: Do you handle commercial carpentry?
faq.answer: Yes. Siong Design supports office, retail, pantry, showroom, gallery and exhibition carpentry works.

faq.question: Are BLUM parts included in the estimate?
faq.answer: BLUM parts are charged separately unless stated in the quotation. Pricing follows BLUM’s Recommended Retail Price from connect.blum.com, plus GST, plus an additional 5% handling and coordination charge.

faq.question: What board and finishing standard do you use?
faq.answer: Our boards come with edge binding, internal and external laminate, E0 formaldehyde-free wood, ABS trimming doors, soft-close drawer options and soft-close hinges based on the confirmed package.

faq.question: Are kitchen top and bottom cabinets counted together?
faq.answer: No. Top cabinet and bottom cabinet are treated as separate carpentry items and are calculated separately by foot run.

faq.question: Why do we use melamine board instead of cheap PVC board?
faq.answer: For most dry interior carpentry, melamine board gives a more solid, premium and cabinet-like finish compared with cheap PVC board. It usually has better screw holding, a wider choice of colours and textures, and feels more suitable for wardrobes, kitchen cabinets, TV consoles and storage cabinets. PVC board can still be useful in wet areas, but cheap PVC boards may feel more hollow, flex more easily and may not look as refined.

faq.question: What type of plywood do we use?
faq.answer: We use different plywood thicknesses depending on the cabinet part and purpose. For backing panels, we use 6mm plywood because it keeps the cabinet neat and lightweight while providing proper rear support. For internal shelving, we use 9mm solid plywood because shelves need better strength and stability to hold daily items.

faq.question: Why do we use 6mm plywood for backing?
faq.answer: 6mm plywood is suitable for cabinet backing because it keeps the cabinet neat and lightweight while still giving the rear panel proper support. It is commonly used for back panels because this area does not carry the same load as shelves.

faq.question: Why do we use 9mm solid plywood for internal shelving?
faq.answer: Internal shelves need to hold daily items, so 9mm solid plywood gives better strength and stability compared with thinner backing material. It helps the shelf feel more solid and reduces flexing during normal use.
